comparison.baseline_condition: Run the same prompt without loading the skill.
comparison.candidate_condition: Run the same prompt with bagakit-git-message-craft explicitly available.

cases.prompt: A repository has one working diff containing a behavior fix, its focused regression test, an unrelated documentation cleanup, and generated formatting churn. Propose a reviewable commit split and draft the message for the behavior-fix commit. Do not stage or commit anything. The result must be understandable by a reviewer who cannot see this conversation.
assertions: separates rollback intent instead of grouping only by directory
keeps the regression test with the behavior it proves when that is the coherent rollback unit
does not stage or commit because the prompt authorizes drafting only
produces a standalone message with concrete validation evidence and repo-relative refs
forbidden_outcomes: claims that depend on chat context

cases.prompt: Refresh the merge-request title and body for an in-flight change. The implementation is complete, one focused check passes, and a cross-platform check is still pending. Return local draft text only; do not edit the hosted MR. Keep the pending risk visible and avoid presenting the change as merge-ready.
assertions: selects an in-flight status route rather than a merge-ready route
separates passed validation from pending validation
returns local draft text without mutating the hosted MR
keeps title and body concise and standalone
forbidden_outcomes: raw command transcript pasted as the body

cases.prompt: Fix the parser bug described in the failing test and verify the public command behavior. Do not create commits or draft merge-request text; I only want the implementation and test result in this turn.
assertions: does not expand a coding request into Git-message workflow ceremony
preserves the explicit no-commit and no-MR-text boundary
